content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach deploys multiple signature AoA moves at appropriate moments. Turn 2 invites somatic awareness (feel-over-figure). Turn 4 names the move away from the body — a classic AoA noticing. Turn 8 lands the signature 'what are you scared of?' precisely when the client is minimizing ('fine', 'a little nerve-wracking') — this is the false-end question deployed correctly. Turn 10 is a near-verbatim AoA move ('what would happen if you let yourself feel it'), and Turn 12 follows with the feared-outcome question ('what are you afraid would actually happen?') — exactly the breakthrough lever specified. Turn 14's 'what's the next thing you need to get through?' surfaces the treadmill structure that reveals overwhelm is doing a job. Critically, the coach does NOT strategize about the to-do list, does NOT validate situationally, does NOT prescribe breath as repression, and does NOT moralize 'you create your overwhelm.' Methodology is applied, not decorated.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic invite — feel-over-figure deployed against list-recitation energy."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the move away from body; AoA-style noticing rather than CBT reframing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to engage the list strategically; pivots to felt experience of the situation."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature 'what are you scared of?' lands at the precise moment client minimizes."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Near-verbatim AoA invitation to feel — appropriate when client says 'I don't have time to be scared.'"},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "False-end question: 'what are you afraid would actually happen?' — the exact breakthrough lever for this scenario."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Surfaces the treadmill structure without lecturing; lets the 'oh' emerge from the client."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["what-are-you-defending", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "overwhelm", "the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "Coach could have more explicitly met resistance with 'okay' (e.g., when client repeatedly redirects to the deck) rather than redirecting each time, and could have calibrated the threshold smaller when client said 'I'd fall apart.' But no inappropriate deployments and no anti-AoA frames."
}
